*   **Unified Order Book:** Tokenized securities will trade on the same order book as traditional securities. They will follow the same execution priority rules to ensure market parity.

*   **Material Rights:** The infrastructure is built to ensure tokenized assets carry the "same material rights and privileges" as traditional securities of an equivalent class, preventing the fragmented ownership models seen in some international markets.

*   **Gemini Investment:** Nasdaq has reportedly planned a **$50 million investment** in the crypto exchange Gemini, signaling a deepening of technical and institutional ties in the digital asset space.

While these funds are technically active, their reporting activity has recently been characterized by "zero-flow" days and a transition to net redemptions.

*   **Institutional vs. Retail Split:** Data indicates that XRP ETFs have a smaller institutional footprint compared to other assets like Solana. Only about **16%** of XRP ETF assets are identifiable via institutional 13F filings, suggesting the investor base is heavily driven by retail "superfans."

## Reasons for the Decline in Momentum
The shift from high demand to stagnant or negative reporting is attributed to several factors:

> **"This transforms static holdings into productive capital while bringing much-needed diversification to DeFi collateral,"** Han added.

[Sentora](https://sentora.com/) is overseeing risk management for the tokenized equity lending market. It defines parameters such as collateral factors, borrow caps, and liquidation thresholds after deep asset-level analysis and ongoing monitoring.

“Retail investors are holding enormous amounts of value in equities that, until now, they haven’t been able to do very much with,” said Sentora CEO Anthony Demartino.

> **“Tokenization changes that by allowing those assets to be used productively, without forcing people to sell and potentially lose long term upside,”** Demartino added.

# ONDO Expands Tokenized Stock Platform to BNB Chain

[By Ayesha Aziz](https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/author/ayesha-aziz)

2m

5mo ago

The platform crossed $350 million in total value locked within weeks of launch and has generated over $669 million in total on-chain volume.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3csvg%20xmlns=%27http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%27%20version=%271.1%27%20width=%27720%27%20height=%27405%27/%3e)![ONDO Expands Tokenized Stock Platform to BNB Chain](https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/b1700fe28e384d4487e91e4246fbd45c.png)

BNB News

Ondo Finance announced the expansion of its tokenized securities platform, Ondo Global Markets, to BNB Chain. The platform provides non-U.S. investors with around-the-clock access to U.S. equities through blockchain-based settlement and custody infrastructure.

Ondo Global Markets launched on Ethereum in September, offering more than 100 tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs on-chain. The platform crossed $350 million in total value locked within weeks of launch and has generated over $669 million in total on-chain volume.

Nathan Allman, CEO of Ondo Finance, stated that BNB Chain hosts one of the largest and most engaged global user bases in Web3. The expansion enables the platform to deliver tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs to millions of users across Asia, Latin America, and other regions in a fast, cost-efficient, and highly interoperable environment.

Peter Thiel's Founders Fund co-led a $20 million Series A funding round for Ondo Finance in 2022. The company followed up with a $10 million public token sale later that summer, establishing its position in the tokenized real-world asset sector.

Earlier this year, Ondo Finance partnered with the Trump-backed World Liberty Financial to advance tokenized RWA adoption. World Liberty Financial stated it would explore integrating Ondo's tokenized assets into the WLFI network as treasury reserve assets.

The company recently completed acquisitions of U.S.-regulated broker Oasis Pro and blockchain developer Strangelove. These moves strengthen Ondo's capabilities in regulated securities infrastructure and blockchain development.

Sarah Song, head of business development at BNB Chain, noted that real-world assets represent one of the fastest-growing segments on the network. She described Ondo Finance joining the ecosystem as strong validation of that momentum.

Ondo Finance earlier this month challenged Nasdaq's proposal to support tokenized equities, requesting more information about settlement procedures. Nasdaq filed a rule change with the SEC in September for tokenized stock versions, arguing that markets can adopt tokenization while maintaining core investor protections. Ondo Finance currently maintains nearly $1.8 billion in total value locked across its platforms.

# Ondo Finance Brings 200+ Tokenized U.S. Stocks and ETFs to Solana

## The expansion comes as tokenized real-world assets continue to gain traction across crypto markets.

By [Margaux Nijkerk](https://www.coindesk.com/author/margaux-nijkerk), [AI Boost](https://www.coindesk.com/author/ai-boost) \|Edited by [Stephen Alpher](https://www.coindesk.com/author/stephen-alpher)

Jan 21, 2026, 9:30 a.m.

![Credit: Shutterstock](https://www.coindesk.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fs3y3vcno%2Fproduction%2F62f409ba891aac91753fc2f4043b8f9f6d271891-1500x1029.jpg%3Fauto%3Dformat&w=3840&q=75)

#### What to know:

- Ondo Finance is expanding its tokenized equities business to Solana, bringing more than 200 tokenized U.S. stocks and exchange-traded funds to the blockchain.
- The move extends the platform, called Ondo Global Markets, beyond Ethereum and BNB Chain, where [it launched in late 2025](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/12/15/ondo-finance-to-offer-tokenized-u-s-stocks-etfs-on-solana-early-next-year).

Ondo Finance is expanding its tokenized equities business to Solana, bringing more than 200 tokenized U.S. stocks and exchange-traded funds to the high-speed blockchain for the first time, the company said Wednesday.

The expansion comes as tokenized real-world assets continue to gain traction across crypto markets, with firms increasingly looking to bring traditional financial products onchain.

![jwp-player-placeholder](https://www.coindesk.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fs3y3vcno%2Fstaging%2Fac6bf763b67c66aeb40bc1b8d64de9e4fea8527f-2000x1500.png&w=3840&q=75)

The move extends Ondo’s platform, called Ondo Global Markets, beyond Ethereum and BNB Chain, [where it launched in late 2025](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/12/15/ondo-finance-to-offer-tokenized-u-s-stocks-etfs-on-solana-early-next-year). With the Solana deployment, Ondo becomes the largest real-world asset (RWA) issuer on the network by asset count, the company claims.

"We've seen early signs of demand for onchain equities on Solana, but liquidity depth and asset selection from the current versions of tokenized stocks remain limited today," said Ian De Bode, president of Ondo Finance, to CoinDesk. "Ondo's tokenized stocks are built to address that by bringing liquidity inherited from traditional exchange venues and a broad catalog of stocks and ETFs onchain."

The launch gives Solana users access to tokenized versions of U.S. equities and ETFs, with trading and discovery supported through existing Solana infrastructure, according to the company.

The Solana rollout significantly broadens the range of tokenized assets available on the network, spanning technology and growth stocks, blue-chip equities, broad-market and sector ETFs, and commodity-linked products. Ondo said the expansion is aimed at making traditional financial assets more accessible through onchain infrastructure, while maintaining price parity with traditional markets.

“We’re excited to bring hundreds of onchain equities with Wall Street liquidity to Solana’s thriving ecosystem,” De Bode said in the press release shared with CoinDesk.

Since launching Ondo Global Markets on Ethereum last year, the platform has expanded rapidly across blockchains, reflecting growing interest in tokenized securities among crypto-native users and institutions alike.

"Expanding to Solana is a natural next step given its scale and performance," De Bode told CoinDesk.

# Ondo Finance Becomes the Largest Provider of Both Tokenized Treasuries & Stocks, Surpassing $2.5 Billion in Total Value Locked

[![](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/8k2tqa6n/production/721e2e3edb6efe6472b4dbad446079199a216cef-200x200.png)\\
\\
Ondo Finance\\
\\
Official Blog](https://ondo.finance/blog)

![Ondo Finance Becomes the Largest Provider of Both Tokenized Treasuries & Stocks](https://ondo.finance/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2F8k2tqa6n%2Fproduction%2F65fc6648bfe82b31fb2c68f85c56b48d9a15eac7-1600x960.png&w=3840&q=75)

![](https://ondo.finance/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fres.cloudinary.com%2Fdaoza8wnr%2Fimage%2Fupload%2Fv1769185067%2FAll_Tokens_but_No_3rd_party_Disclaimer-1_mdlveg.png&w=3840&q=75)

- _Ondo is now the #1 provider of tokenized Treasuries by TVL, asset holders, and partner integrations_
- _Ondo tokenized stocks surpass $500M in TVL, exceeding all other platforms combined, with over $7B in cumulative volume in less than 6 months since launching in September_
- _Ondo products are also the most widely held across categories, with user numbers in the tens of thousands, and feature the largest number of DeFi integrations_
- _Growth is driven by accelerating adoption across platforms and chains including Bitget, Binance Wallet, Solana, Ethereum, BNB Chain and more._

Ondo Finance, a leading platform for tokenized real-world assets, announced today that it has surpassed $2.5 billion in total value locked (TVL) across its tokenized products, cementing its position as the largest platform for both tokenized U.S. Treasuries and tokenized stocks, per [RWA.xyz](https://app.rwa.xyz/treasuries) & [DefiLlama](https://defillama.com/protocols/rwa).

### Market Leadership in Tokenized U.S. Treasuries

Ondo now leads the tokenized Treasury market with approximately $2 billion in TVL across its two products, the highest of any tokenized asset provider. This growth is driven by [USDY](https://ondo.finance/usdy), Ondo’s permissionless tokenized Treasury product, which has surpassed $1 billion in TVL. USDY is available across nine blockchains, enabling global (non-U.S.) individual and institutional investors to access a stablecoin-like experience combined with yield.

In parallel, [OUSG](https://ondo.finance/ousg), Ondo’s flagship institutional fund, has grown to over $770M in TVL, offering 24/7 subscriptions and redemptions, daily interest accrual, low fees, and multi-chain support across Ethereum, Solana, XRP Ledger, and Polygon. Its portfolio spans tokenized funds from leading asset managers, including Fidelity (FDIT), BlackRock (BUIDL), Franklin Templeton (BENJI), WisdomTree (WTGXX), and Wellington Management and FundBridge Capital’s (ULTRA), with State Street Investment Management’s SWEEP coming soon.

### The Largest Platform for Tokenized Stocks

Ondo is also the largest platform for tokenized stocks, representing more than 50% of the total market share and exceeding all other platforms combined by total value locked, despite having the lowest time in market. Since launching in September 2025, Ondo Global Markets has now surpassed $500 million in total value across 200+ tokenized stocks with tens of thousands of asset holders driving over $7 billion in cumulative trading volume. Ondo tokenized stocks are now live across Solana, Ethereum, and BNB Chain, and supported by leading wallets, exchanges, custodians, and protocols. and are supported by many major wallets, custodians, and exchanges, including Binance Wallet, Bitget, Trust Wallet, OKX Wallet, Blockchain.com, and more.

**About Ondo Finance**
Ondo Finance is a blockchain-based platform focused on tokenizing real-world assets and bringing institutional-quality financial products onchain. By bridging traditional finance and decentralized infrastructure, Ondo aims to make capital markets more accessible, transparent, and efficient.

# Ondo partners with Chainlink to bring tokenized stocks to DeFi

![](https://s.yimg.com/ny/api/res/1.2/wfOU8Im0lYqd5wbrOWh45w--/YXBwaWQ9aGlnaGxhbmRlcjt3PTk2MDtoPTU0MDtjZj13ZWJw/https://media.zenfs.com/en/thestreet_881/90acee722801268335264a63907db2b3)

Anand Sinha

11 February 20263 min read

[ONDO-USD \\
-6.14%](https://sg.finance.yahoo.com/quote/ONDO-USD/ "ONDO-USD")

[ETH-USD \\

Popular tokenization platform [Ondo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ondo-brings-tokenized-u-stocks-220000691.html) announced on Feb. 11 that [Ondo Global Markets](https://ondo.finance/global-markets) has integrated [Chainlink](https://chain.link/) [Data Feeds](https://docs.chain.link/data-feeds) for its tokenized stocks and ETFs live on the [Ethereum](https://thestreet.com/crypto/ethereum) mainnet.

Institutional-grade data for SPYon, QQQon, and TSLAon are now onchain. Data feeds for all assets will go live soon.

This is the first time tokenized stocks are available for use on Ethereum DeFi. Not only that, Ondo tokenized stocks can be reliably used as productive DeFi collateral for the first time.

Beyond being held and traded, tokenized stocks can now be deployed across lending markets, vaults, and structured products to access yields.

With institutional-grade liquidity and oracle infrastructure, Ondo tokenized stocks could revolutionize the DeFi economy.

**[Related: What is tokenization? Explained](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/tokenization-explained-173057294.html)**

## Traders can now deploy tokenized stocks as productive capital

So far, tokenized securities couldn't be used like traditional finance offerings as collateral for lending and borrowing.

For DeFi protocols to accept them as collateral, tokenized stocks must first have:

1. Deep, reliable liquidity to support near-instant liquidations without significant slippage

2. High-integrity, real-time pricing data that reflects all economic events


While Ondo tokenized stocks and ETFs have inherited deep, efficient liquidity directly from traditional equity venues such as Nasdaq and NYSE, they earlier lacked the required institutional-grade onchain data.

Ondo's integration with Chainlink addresses the concern as institutional-grade pricing tailored specifically for Ondo’s tokenized equities is now onchain.

Each data feed reflects the full economic reality of the underlying asset, including corporate actions such as dividends. This way, DeFi protocols can accurately price Ondo Global Markets assets, safely manage positions, and protect traders during volatile market conditions.

**[Related: What are tokenized stocks? Explained](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/tokenized-stocks-explained-183016738.html)**

## Lending markets for tokenized stocks now live

Following the latest collaboration, Ondo Global Markets assets are going to be integrated into DeFi lending markets.

With initial lending support for SPYon, QQQon, and TSLAon live on [Euler](https://www.euler.finance/), users can borrow stablecoins against Ondo tokenized stocks.

"DeFi shouldn’t stop at crypto. By enabling tokenized stocks as collateral on Euler users can borrow against their equities and unlock liquidity without selling," said Euler CEO Jonathan Han.

> **"This transforms static holdings into productive capital while bringing much-needed diversification to DeFi collateral,"** Han added.

Story continues

[Sentora](https://sentora.com/) is overseeing risk management for the tokenized equity lending market. It defines parameters such as collateral factors, borrow caps, and liquidation thresholds after deep asset-level analysis and ongoing monitoring.

“Retail investors are holding enormous amounts of value in equities that, until now, they haven’t been able to do very much with,” said Sentora CEO Anthony Demartino.

> **“Tokenization changes that by allowing those assets to be used productively, without forcing people to sell and potentially lose long term upside,”** Demartino added.

This modular, risk-isolated approach provides a controlled environment for introducing tokenized stocks and ETFs into DeFi, while unlocking meaningful capital efficiency for users.

- [**Ondo brings tokenized U.S. stocks and commodities to MetaMask**](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ondo-brings-tokenized-u-stocks-220000691.html)


## New DeFi use cases for tokenized stocks

Ondo Global Markets assets are positioned to support a broader range of DeFi use cases beyond lending markets, the statement read.

Tokenized equities will expand across additional money markets, vaults, and structured products for more efficient capital deployment and portfolio construction.

As integrations grow, Ondo Global Markets assets can serve as core collateral primitives alongside crypto-native assets, helping bridge traditional capital markets and DeFi.

# Convera and Ripple empower stablecoin-enabled cross-border payments

Convera and Ripple announce a strategic partnership to deliver crypto-enabled payment and treasury solutions for cross-border payments in 2026.

March 31, 2026

- [Copy link](https://convera.com/blog/company-news/ripple-convera-stablecoin-enabled-global-payments/ "Copy link")
- [Share on LinkedIn](https://convera.com/blog/company-news/ripple-convera-stablecoin-enabled-global-payments/ "Share on LinkedIn")
- [Share on Twitter](https://convera.com/blog/company-news/ripple-convera-stablecoin-enabled-global-payments/ "Share on Twitter")

![](https://convera.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CompanyNews_BlogHero_2026_p1.jpg)

[Global](https://convera.com/blog/region/global/) [Cryptocurrencies](https://convera.com/blog/tag/cryptocurrencies/) [Stablecoins](https://convera.com/blog/tag/stablecoins/)

Convera, a global leader in commercial payments, has announced a new strategic collaboration with [Ripple,](https://ripple.com/) a leading provider of blockchain-based enterprise solutions across traditional and digital finance, to offer crypto-enabled payment and treasury solutions for businesses.

“With the growing presence and use of digital currencies such as crypto and stablecoins, Convera has maintained a thoughtful approach by listening to what our customers want while watching this space continue to mature. We knew we needed a trusted, visionary partner that can help us meet our customers where they are in their journey,” said [Patrick Gauthier, CEO, Convera](https://convera.com/about-us/leadership/). “Ripple is a clear leader in the crypto space and a natural fit for Convera. We look forward to continued success and growth as we roll out these capabilities to customers near and far.”

Convera’s partnership with Ripple brings together two industry leaders to enhance global payments through stablecoin and blockchain infrastructure. By combining Convera’s trusted global network, FX expertise, and customer experience with Ripple’s liquidity, settlement, and digital asset capabilities, the collaboration enables faster, more reliable cross‑border payments, particularly in corridors where traditional options are limited.

This partnership builds on the “ [stablecoin sandwich](https://convera.com/blog/cross-border-payments/stablecoin-sandwich-and-cross-border-payments/)” settlement model, where payments begin and end in fiat while leveraging regulated stablecoins for settlement in between. Convera orchestrates the end-to-end payment experience, while Ripple provides the underlying infrastructure for liquidity, on/off-ramping, and cross-border settlement.

“Enterprises are increasingly looking for faster, more flexible ways to move money globally without taking on the complexity of digital assets directly,” said [Aaron Slettehaugh, SVP of Product at Ripple](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aaron-slettehaugh/). “By partnering with Convera, we’re combining a trusted global payment infrastructure with stablecoin-powered settlement to give businesses more control over how and when they move value across borders.”

Sign up to receive Convera’s upcoming [Payments 2026+: Liquidity in Motion Report,](https://convera.com/lp/payments-2026-report/) which outlines how accelerating regulatory deadlines, real-time payment innovation, and the emergence of multi rail ecosystems are redefining global currency management.

Ripple Backs Convera To Enabled Cross Border Stablecoin Payments

Convera, the commercial payments firm formerly known as Western Union Business Solutions, has partnered with Ripple to integrate blockchain technology into its global payment network, covering 140 currencies across 200 countries.

**The "Stablecoin Sandwich" Model**

To streamline cross-border transfers, the companies are using a settlement method called the "stablecoin sandwich." This model, Convera says, offers the speed of blockchain without requiring businesses to handle digital assets directly while stablecoins, including Ripple’s RLUSD (CRYPTO: $RLUSD) will serve as the intermediary settlement layer.

This partnership follows Ripple’s recent expansion into institutional finance. Last week, Ripple took part in the Singapore central bank’s BLOOM initiative to test programmable cross-border trade settlements using the XRP Ledger along with RLUSD stablecoins.

# XRP News: Ripple  Partners With Convera for Stablecoin Cross-Border Payments

Mar 31, 2026, 10:24 GMT-42 min read

[![](https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/crypto/XTVCXRP.svg)XRPUSD−0.16%](https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/XRPUSD/?exchange=BITSTAMP)

Ripple [![](https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/crypto/XTVCXRP.svg)XRPUSD](https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/BITSTAMP-XRPUSD/) News: Convera has announced a strategic collaboration with Ripple to introduce stablecoin-enabled cross-border payments for businesses. The partnership combines Convera’s global payment network with Ripple’s blockchain infrastructure to deliver faster settlements, improved liquidity, and more flexible treasury solutions using crypto and stablecoins.

**Convera Partners Ripple for Stablecoin Payments**

According to the announcement, the collaboration focuses on offering crypto-enabled payment and treasury services for enterprises. By integrating Ripple’s infrastructure, Convera aims to simplify international transfers and reduce delays associated with traditional banking systems.

The collaboration also targets payment corridors where legacy financial rails remain slow or costly. Businesses using the platform will be able to move funds more efficiently while maintaining familiar fiat-based workflows.

Patrick Gauthier, CEO of Convera, said Ripple is a clear leader in the crypto space and a natural fit for the company.

“We look forward to continued success and growth as we roll out these capabilities to customers near and far.”

**Stablecoin “Sandwich” Model Powers Faster Settlement**

The collaboration uses a stablecoin-based settlement structure often called the “stablecoin sandwich” model. In this setup, payments start in fiat currency, convert into stablecoins for settlement, and then convert back into fiat at the receiving end.

Convera manages the full payment flow, including customer experience and compliance. Meanwhile, Ripple provides the backend infrastructure, including liquidity sourcing, on- and off-ramps, and cross-border settlement.

This approach allows companies to move funds globally without exposure to crypto volatility. It also reduces reliance on multiple intermediary banks, which typically slow down international transfers.

**Enterprises Demand Faster Global Money Movement**

The move comes as businesses increasingly look for more efficient ways to send funds internationally. Stablecoin settlement offers quicker processing times and more predictable costs compared to traditional cross-border payments.

Aaron Slettehaugh, SVP of Product at Ripple, “Enterprises are increasingly looking for faster, more flexible ways to move money globally without taking on the complexity of digital assets directly.”

By combining Convera’s payment expertise with Ripple’s blockchain-based liquidity, the partnership aims to give businesses more control over how and when funds move across borders.

The collaboration highlights growing adoption of stablecoins in enterprise finance. As demand rises, infrastructure partnerships like this could play a key role in reshaping global payment systems.

# Convera and Ripple Forge Transformative Partnership to Revolutionize Stablecoin Payment Infrastructure

[Blockchain](https://cryptorank.io/news/tag/blockchain) [Fintech](https://cryptorank.io/news/tag/fintech) [Banking](https://cryptorank.io/news/tag/banking) [Payments](https://cryptorank.io/news/tag/payments)

forBitcoin World

Share:

![Visualization of Convera and Ripple's enhanced global stablecoin payment network connecting financial markets.](https://bitcoinworld.co.in/wp-content/uploads/convera-ripple-stablecoin-payments-partnership-803x600.jpg)

[BitcoinWorld](https://bitcoinworld.co.in/)

![](https://bitcoinworld.co.in/wp-content/uploads/convera-ripple-stablecoin-payments-partnership.jpg)

[Convera and Ripple Forge Transformative Partnership to Revolutionize Stablecoin Payment Infrastructure](https://bitcoinworld.co.in/convera-ripple-stablecoin-payments-partnership/)

Global payments firm Convera has announced a strategic partnership with blockchain enterprise Ripple to fundamentally enhance its stablecoin-based payment capabilities, marking a significant development in cross-border financial infrastructure announced on March 31, 2025. This collaboration directly addresses longstanding industry challenges surrounding transaction speed, liquidity management, and settlement reliability in international finance. Consequently, the partnership signals a maturation phase for enterprise blockchain adoption within traditional payment corridors.

## Convera and Ripple Partnership Details

The core objective of the Convera and Ripple partnership centers on integrating Ripple’s established global payment network with Convera’s extensive financial settlement infrastructure. Specifically, the companies plan to leverage stablecoin technology as a primary settlement layer. This integration aims to create a more efficient financial pipeline. For instance, transactions that previously required multiple intermediary banks can now potentially settle directly. Therefore, the partnership targets measurable improvements in three key operational areas: transaction finality speed, liquidity optimization, and system reliability.

Industry analysts immediately recognized the strategic significance of this move. Convera, formerly known as Western Union Business Solutions, processes billions in cross-border transactions annually for businesses and institutions. Meanwhile, Ripple provides enterprise-grade blockchain solutions through its RippleNet network and XRP Ledger. By combining a traditional payments powerhouse with a leading blockchain provider, the alliance creates a hybrid model. This model potentially offers the regulatory compliance of traditional finance with the technological efficiency of digital assets.

## Stablecoin Payment Capabilities Enhanced

The enhancement of stablecoin payment capabilities represents the technical cornerstone of this collaboration. Stablecoins are digital currencies pegged to stable assets like the US dollar. They offer the programmability and speed of cryptocurrency without the volatility of assets like Bitcoin. Convera will utilize these digital instruments to settle transactions on Ripple’s network. This method contrasts sharply with traditional systems like SWIFT, which often involve pre-funded nostro accounts in destination countries, tying up significant capital.

The partnership will likely focus on major stablecoins such as USD Coin (USDC) and PayPal USD (PYUSD), which are widely recognized and regulated. Utilizing these assets allows for near-instant settlement and 24/7 operational capacity. Furthermore, it reduces foreign exchange risk and counterparty exposure during the settlement process. The table below outlines the projected improvements:

| Metric | Traditional System | Stablecoin Network (Projected) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Settlement Time** | 2-5 business days | Minutes to hours |
| **Liquidity Requirement** | High (pre-funded accounts) | Reduced (on-demand settlement) |
| **Operational Window** | Business hours, timezone dependent | 24/7/365 |
| **Transaction Transparency** | Limited, opaque tracking | Enhanced, near real-time tracking |

### Expert Analysis on Market Impact

Financial technology experts view this partnership as a validation of blockchain’s utility in wholesale finance. “The Convera-Ripple collaboration is not about displacing traditional currency,” notes Dr. Anya Sharma, a fintech researcher at the Cambridge Centre for Alternative Finance. “Instead, it’s about creating a more efficient settlement rail between regulated entities. This model uses stablecoins as a bridge asset, not as a consumer-facing currency. Consequently, it sidesteps many regulatory hurdles associated with retail crypto payments while delivering tangible efficiency gains.”

This analysis aligns with broader trends in institutional finance. Major banks and payment processors are increasingly experimenting with distributed ledger technology for back-office settlement. The partnership provides a concrete use case with immediate scale, given Convera’s existing client base. Moreover, it arrives amid a global push for faster payment systems, such as the Federal Reserve’s FedNow in the United States and the SEPA Instant scheme in Europe. The stablecoin-based network could complement these systems by providing an alternative for specific high-value or cross-currency corridors.

## Global Payment Network Evolution

The evolution of the global payment network is accelerating through such integrations. RippleNet already connects hundreds of financial institutions worldwide. Convera’s integration adds a significant node with deep liquidity and regulatory expertise in numerous jurisdictions. This synergy could encourage other large payment service providers (PSPs) and money transfer operators (MTOs) to adopt similar hybrid models. Therefore, the partnership may serve as a blueprint for the industry.

The technical implementation will likely involve Ripple’s Liquidity Hub product or similar solutions. These platforms allow enterprises to source digital assets from multiple liquidity venues. They also manage the conversion between fiat and stablecoins seamlessly. For Convera’s clients, the front-end experience may remain unchanged—a business initiates a payment in euros to a supplier in Mexico. However, the back-end settlement could occur via a USDC transfer on the XRP Ledger, converted to Mexican pesos at the destination. This process eliminates several intermediate steps and associated costs.

- **Reduced Operational Cost:** Lower fees from eliminating correspondent banks.
- **Enhanced Speed:** Settlements finalize in minutes, not days.
- **Improved Transparency:** All parties track the payment in real-time.
- **Better Liquidity Management:** Capital is not trapped in nostro accounts.

### Regulatory Landscape and Compliance

A critical factor for the partnership’s success will be navigating the complex regulatory landscape. Both companies have extensive experience in financial compliance. Convera operates under money transmitter licenses globally. Ripple has engaged with regulators through its ongoing legal proceedings and compliance initiatives. Their joint approach will likely emphasize that they are using regulated stablecoins issued by licensed entities like Circle and Paxos. Furthermore, they will implement robust Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks at both ends of a transaction.

This compliant framework is essential for gaining trust from corporate treasurers and financial institutions. The model does not propose a permissionless, public blockchain for settlement. Instead, it uses a controlled, enterprise-grade network where all participants are vetted financial entities. This structure aligns with regulatory expectations for the “Travel Rule” and other financial surveillance requirements. As a result, the partnership may face fewer regulatory headwinds than consumer-focused crypto payment projects.

## Conclusion

The partnership between Convera and Ripple represents a pivotal step in the modernization of global payment infrastructure. By focusing on stablecoin-based settlement, the alliance targets concrete improvements in speed, cost, and reliability for cross-border transactions. This collaboration provides a scalable model for integrating blockchain technology into traditional finance, emphasizing compliance and enterprise utility. As the initiative progresses, its success could catalyze further adoption of digital asset settlement rails by other major payment processors, fundamentally reshaping how global commerce moves money. The Convera and Ripple partnership, therefore, stands as a significant marker in the ongoing convergence of cryptocurrency innovation and established financial services.

## FAQs

**Q1:** What is the primary goal of the Convera and Ripple partnership?

The primary goal is to enhance Convera’s global payment capabilities by integrating Ripple’s network and using stablecoins for settlement, aiming to improve transaction speed, reduce liquidity requirements, and increase reliability.

**Q2:** How will stablecoins be used in this new payment system?

Stablecoins like USDC will act as a bridge settlement asset. Convera will convert sender funds into a stablecoin, transmit it over Ripple’s network, and convert it to the destination currency, enabling faster and cheaper settlement compared to traditional correspondent banking.

**Q3:** Will this partnership affect everyday consumers using Convera’s services?

Initially, the focus is likely on business-to-business (B2B) and institutional payments. Consumers may benefit indirectly through potentially lower fees and faster service times if the technology is rolled out across Convera’s broader platforms in the future.

**Q4:** What are the regulatory implications of using stablecoins for cross-border payments?

Both companies emphasize using regulated stablecoins and maintaining strict KYC/AML compliance. The model is designed to work within existing financial regulations by partnering with licensed stablecoin issuers and operating as vetted financial entities on a controlled network.

**Q5:** How does this partnership differ from other blockchain payment projects?

This partnership is distinctive due to the scale of the participants—a major global payments firm and an established enterprise blockchain provider. It focuses specifically on the back-end settlement layer for institutional flows rather than creating a new consumer-facing payment app or cryptocurrency.

# Ripple Taps Former Western Union Unit Convera for Stablecoin Payments

[By Ayesha Aziz](https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/author/ayesha-aziz)

2m

2d ago

Convera handles transactions in more than 140 currencies and operates across roughly 200 countries and territories.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3csvg%20xmlns=%27http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%27%20version=%271.1%27%20width=%27720%27%20height=%27405%27/%3e)![Ripple Taps Former Western Union Unit Convera for Stablecoin Payments](https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/39c2735948f8471fb597d2aedeb20a7b.jpg)

#### Table of Contents

- [Ripple News](https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/article/ripple-taps-former-western-union-unit-convera-for-stablecoin-payments#ripple-news)

## Ripple News

Ripple Labs and Convera [announced](https://convera.com/blog/company-news/western-union-business-solutions-to-be-renamed-convera/) a cross-border payments partnership on Tuesday. The two companies said they will use stablecoin and blockchain technology to improve how money moves across borders. Convera handles transactions in more than 140 currencies and operates across roughly 200 countries and territories.

The company was formerly known as Western Union Business Solutions. A private equity consortium acquired it for $910 million in 2021, after which it was rebranded as Convera. Its current CEO, Patrick Gauthier, previously led Amazon Pay before joining the fintech firm.

At the core of the partnership is a structure the two companies call a "stablecoin sandwich." Payments start and finish in fiat currency, but stablecoins handle the transfer in between. The model is designed to give enterprises faster settlement without requiring them to hold or manage digital assets directly.

Aaron Slettehaugh, Ripple's senior vice president of product, said enterprises want speed and flexibility when moving money globally. He added that most of these businesses do not want to take on the complexity of managing digital assets themselves. The stablecoin sandwich model addresses that by keeping the experience familiar on both ends.

Gauthier said Ripple stood out as a partner because of its position in the crypto payments market. "Ripple is a clear leader in the crypto space and a natural fit for Convera," he [said](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260331576971/en/Convera-Joins-Forces-with-Ripple-to-Empower-Stablecoin-Enabled-Cross-Border-Payments) in a statement. He also noted that Convera has been watching the digital currency space mature while listening to what its customers need.

Ripple developed and maintains the XRP Ledger and issues the [RLUSD](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/ripple-usd/) stablecoin, which is pegged to the U.S. dollar. Last week, the company joined the Singapore central bank's BLOOM initiative. That program is piloting programmable cross-border trade settlements using the XRP Ledger and [RLUSD](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/ripple-usd/).

The Convera deal follows that Singapore announcement and continues Ripple's pattern of expanding through targeted partnerships. Convera's reach across nearly every major currency market gives the arrangement immediate operational scale. The companies did not disclose financial terms or confirm when the first live payment corridors under the agreement will launch.
